## VISÃO GERAL

O Ethical Influence Practitioner Program (EIPP) é o programa de certificação do Cialdini Institute, desenhado para transformar profissionais em "Sleuths of Influence" — praticantes éticos dos 7 Princípios Universais de Persuasão. Diferente de cursos acadêmicos, foca em implementação prática com Personal Action Plans.

## ESTRUTURA DO PROGRAMA

### Módulos do Curso (01-54, 173 arquivos, 248 KB)

| Módulo | Tema | Aulas | Conteúdo Chave |
|--------|------|-------|----------------|
| 1 | Introduction | 3 | Welcome, Why Study Persuasion, Principles Overview |

| 3 | Contrast | 2 | Contrast Phenomenon, Always Available |
| 4 | Reciprocity | 6 | Gifts vs Rewards, Amplifiers (MUC), Concessions, Moments of Power |
| 5 | Liking | 6 | Similarity, Praise, Cooperation, Like-First Rule |
| 6 | Unity | 7 | Kinship, Localism, Acting Together, Co-creation, Ask for Advice |
| 7 | Social Proof | 6 | Activators, Reviews (89%/4.2-4.7), Amplifiers, Future Social Proof |
| 8 | Authority | 5 | AN vs IN Authority, Expertise, Trustworthiness, Weakness-First Strategy |
| 9 | Consistency | 7 | Commitment, Active/Public/Voluntary Amplifiers |
| 10 | Scarcity | 7 | Loss Framing, Competition, Exclusive Information, Double Whammy |
| 11 | Ethics Revisited | 1 | Madoff case, True/Natural/Wise revisited |

## KEY FRAMEWORKS EXTRAÍDOS

### Master Framework: 7 Principles + Activators + Amplifiers

| Principle | Core Motive | Activators | Amplifiers |
|-----------|------------|------------|------------|
| Contrast | Universal | "What you do before" | "Compared to what?" |
| Reciprocity | Relationship | Gifts, Concessions | Meaningful, Unexpected, Customized |
| Liking | Relationship | Similarity, Praise, Cooperation | Like them first |
| Unity | Relationship | Localism, Acting Together, Co-creation | Intensity, Frequency, Ask for Advice |
| Social Proof | Uncertainty | Evidence of others' behavior | Many, Similar, Uncertainty |
| Authority | Uncertainty | Cues (clothes, titles, accomplishments) | Expertise + Trustworthiness, Weakness-first |
| Consistency | Action | Commitment | Active, Public, Voluntary |
| Scarcity | Action | Rare/dwindling availability | Loss framing, Competition, Exclusive info |

### Other Key Frameworks
1. Neidert's Core Motive Model (3 goals → 7 principles)
2. 4-Step Influence Process (Step Back → Think → Solicit → Plan)
3. 3 Criteria for Ethical Influence (True, Natural, Wise)
4. 3 Types of Practitioners (Bungler, Smuggler, Sleuth)
5. Triple Tumor of Organizational Dishonesty
6. Weakness-First Strategy (weakness → "but" → strengths)
7. Stacking Protocol (optimal 2, max 3 principles)
8. Pre-suasion methodology
9. Scarcity Double Whammy
10. Small Bigs (smallest change → biggest ROI)

## KEY STATISTICS EXTRAÍDAS

| Metric | Value | Study |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Review sweet spot | 4.2-4.7/5 stars, 89% positive | Review optimization research |
| Gift vs Reward | 52% vs 23% (gift 8x more cost-effective) | Construction manager survey |
| Weakness-first charity | +36% donations | 2008-2010 recession charity |
| Avis campaign | +700% market share | "We're #2 but try harder" |
| Contrast effect (hot tubs) | +520% sales | San Diego Spa Company |
| Public vs private goals | 86% vs 14% success | UNC Probation study |
| "Will you?" effect | 30% → 10% no-shows | Gordon Sinclair restaurant |
| Scarcity double whammy | 6x sales increase | Australian beef study |
| Tax social proof (town) | 83% compliance | HM Revenue "5B Pound Letter" |
| Active commitment (billboard) | 76% vs 17% | Drive Safely study |
| Voluntary choices | 50% → 5% cancellation | Healthcare conference |
| Loss aversion ratio | 2:1 | Kahneman Prospect Theory |
